scope: Implementation-surface decision record for the compressed shared-KV dKV many-to-one accumulation wall on MI350.
why_this_exists: The current dot_k_sparse and dot_k_slot_sparse Triton branches reach the coalesced-write accounting target at small shapes but are rejected because their local sparse accumulation body is scalar-shaped. The next prototype needs a backend that can express a query-major, D16, segmented/MFMA-friendly route reduction rather than another per-selected Triton loop.

tilelang.read: TileLang is not installed in the current TorchTitan/MORI image, so a TileLang implementation would require adding a dependency or switching images before it can be the first production branch.

aiter.read: AITER is present and has MoE/GMM and grouped row-reduction utilities, but the inspected surfaces are serving/MoE reductions rather than the sparse-attention training dKV route accumulation needed here. A broad package walk also triggers JIT compilation and can fail without an active Triton driver, so use source-file inspection or narrow imports.

compile_smoke.negative_control: The same broad-header smoke without an explicit MI350 arch defaulted to gfx906 and failed in rocPRIM texture-cache iterator host memset resolution; use --offload-arch=gfx950 for MI350 smokes.

pytorch_extension_smoke.read: The actual PyTorch HIP extension build/call surface works in the device-enabled MORI image with CK-Tile, rocPRIM, and hipCUB headers included. The kernel is a copy smoke, not a dKV accumulator.

first_accumulator_smoke.read: This is the first actual compressed-dKV accumulator through the HIP extension surface. It consumes the materialized diagnostic tensors and active-group lists, emits the real grad_kv tensor, and passes S128/S512 parity. The scalar body is rejected as an optimization path at S512, so the next body must replace it with CK-Tile/MFMA-friendly segmented accumulation.

tile_partial_no_atomic_followup.read: This explicitly removes the final global atomic from the scalar HIP accumulator by emitting one [seq_tile, compressed_group, D] partial row and reducing across seq tiles. It passes parity but does not improve S128/S512 timing; S512 remains about 7.3 ms. The immediate bottleneck is therefore the scalar selected-loop/local contraction body, not only the final global atomic instruction. The production body needs MFMA/vectorized selected-key contraction plus in-tile segmented reduction, not a dense partial slab.

backend_decision.preferred_first_surface: HIP C++ extension using CK-Tile block/tile primitives plus rocPRIM/hipCUB segmented-reduction patterns where useful.
backend_decision.fallback_surface: A new Triton kernel is still acceptable for metadata packing and small reductions, but the rejected branches and the HIP scalar smoke show the production body must avoid scalar per-selected active-list loops.

not_first_surface.reason: Not installed in the current MI350 TorchTitan/MORI image.
It reduces K rows per group for MoE/split-K row reductions; it does not directly match the shared-KV dKV route contraction without materializing partial rows.

not_first_surface.surface: external rocPRIM reduce_by_key over materialized partials
not_first_surface.reason: Materialized compact partials are already negative: production must reduce before writing the large partial buffer.

preserve: no selected K/V or probability saved tensor contract
selected-key batched dot-K mechanism where it helps
first_validation_ladder: Compile HIP/CK-Tile extension for gfx950 with a tiny no-op kernel and PyTorch binding.
Implement a synthetic S128 segmented D16 accumulator that consumes materialized probs/grad_logits and active-group lists, matching the existing reference.

Scale to S512 and require beating dot_k_sparse/dot_k_slot_sparse. The first scalar HIP body passes parity but is rejected at S512; the no-global-atomic tile-partial variant also passes but remains around 7.3 ms, so keep the HIP extension surface and replace the scalar body.
Run S4096 timing-only against the dot_k_atomic 8.806 ms and query-major full-atomic 11.081 ms baselines.
Only after accumulator parity/timing is favorable, fuse score/prob/grad-logit recompute and LI/compressor gradients.

decision: Proceed with the gfx950 HIP/CK-Tile/rocPRIM segmented accumulator surface, but do not promote the first scalar HIP body or the no-global-atomic tile-partial scalar variant. The next cycle should replace that scalar body with a CK-Tile/MFMA-friendly reduction schedule rather than polishing row lookup or writing external partial reductions.
